subject: CRITICAL — PANEL_BASIC_AUTH credentials were leaked in APK source comment; rotation REQUIRED
purpose: Phase 3.2 security audit on kernel-apk source-v2 found the operator's literal Panel credentials (PANEL_USER=andrew, PANEL_PASS=<redacted>, plus base64) in a comment block at PanelPusher.kt:56-58. The CODE was externalized to BuildConfig at v0.96.43, but the comment never got scrubbed. Comment is now scrubbed (commit on agent/sinister-kernel-apk/crispy-cosmos-resume this turn). HOWEVER: the credential pair must be considered burned (decompile + git-history grep both expose it; even if Sinister-APK repo is private, defense-in-depth requires rotation).
what_kernel_apk_did_this_turn: Scrubbed the leaked comment in PanelPusher.kt:56-66 (behavior unchanged; BuildConfig path was already canonical)
This inbox to surface the rotation requirement

p0_rotate_panel_credentials.action: Generate new PANEL_USER + PANEL_PASS pair (suggest random 32-char password)
p0_rotate_panel_credentials.rotation_pattern: Deploy panel with BOTH old + new accepted (transition window ~24h), then APK re-deploys with new creds in BuildConfig, then panel removes old from accepted-list
p0_rotate_panel_credentials.blast_radius_if_not_done: Anyone with access to source/decompile (e.g. an attacker who cloned the private repo if it leaked, OR a Leo-machine clone that ends up shared) can authenticate as operator against panel

p1_rotate_apk_fleet_secret.action: Same pattern for DEFAULT_APK_FLEET_SECRET = 'sinister-apk-fleet-2026-05-10' (PanelPusher.kt:55). Move to BuildConfig.APK_FLEET_SECRET, accept both old + new on panel for transition, re-deploy APKs.
p1_rotate_apk_fleet_secret.blast_radius: Attacker can push arbitrary bundles to /api/accounts/push-token spoofing as fleet APK

p2_audit_panel_logs_for_credential_misus.action: Grep last 30 days of panel access logs for unexpected /api/* calls authenticated as PANEL_USER=andrew from non-fleet IPs
p2_audit_panel_logs_for_credential_misus.expected_outcome: ZERO unexpected hits IF the credentials were never actually used by an attacker

fix_pattern_to_emulate: v0.96.43 already moved DEFAULT_BASIC_AUTH from hardcoded literal -> BuildConfig.PANEL_BASIC_AUTH via local.properties injection (gitignored). Same exact pattern for APK_FLEET_SECRET + PANEL_URL constants. See PanelPusher.kt:66 for the canonical approach.
